reasoning: The resolution criteria asks if Anish Giri will have a ranking "as high or higher" than on 2025-03-16. His current rank on March 1, 2025 is #19. For March 23, the monthly update will not have happened yet, so Wikipedia will almost certainly still show him at #19. For April and beyond, his ranking will reflect the March 'Prague Chess Festival' where he performed decently but lost to Aravindh Chithambaram, who had a phenomenal 2830 TPR tournament and jumped to #14 in the world live rankings. Aravindh jumping ahead pushes everyone below #14 down by one spot. Meanwhile, Le Quang Liem (formerly #18) performed poorly and likely drops below Giri. This creates a tight race where Giri could either be barely #19 or drop to #20/#21. Because he lost absolute Elo points and was overtaken by a rising Aravindh, it is more likely than not that he falls to #20 or lower on the April list. Over longer horizons, Giri's age (slightly past his peak compared to new rising Indian stars) naturally pushes his probability of maintaining a top 19 spot slowly downwards, though his high floor keeps him competitive.
